title: Cyclosorus × altissimus
intro: Cyclosorus × altissimus, also known as the giant alpine fern, is a hybrid plant species within the family Thelypteridaceae. It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties that can aid in reducing swelling, protecting against cell damage, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus × altissimus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Cyclosorus × altissimus include teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as dried extracts and capsules, which are used to support overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus × altissimus is a hybrid of the genera Cyclosorus and possibly other related species. It is classified as a member of the family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. The genus Cyclosorus is part of the larger division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The classification of Cyclosorus × altissimus is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf structure and reproductive features.
